anchor.snippet: two points x and y in X are topologically distinguishable
Two points x and y are separated if each of them has a neighbourhood that is not a neighbourhood of the other
two subsets A and B of X are separated if each is disjoint from the other's closure
Subsets A and B are separated by neighbourhoods if they have disjoint neighbourhoods
They are separated by closed neighbourhoods if they have disjoint closed neighbourhoods
They are separated by a continuous function if there exists a continuous function f from the space X to the real line

annotations.label: Precisely separated by a continuous function

anchor.snippet: they are precisely separated by a continuous function if there exists a continuous function f from X to
or Kolmogorov , if any two distinct points in X are topologically distinguishable
or symmetric , if any two topologically distinguishable points in X are separated
or accessible or Fréchet , if any two distinct points in X are separated
Thus, X is T 1 if and only if it is both T 0 and R 0
or preregular , if any two topologically distinguishable points in X are separated by neighbourhoods
Every R 1 space is also R 0
is Hausdorff , or T 2 or separated , if any two distinct points in X are separated by neighbourhoods
Thus, X is Hausdorff if and only if it is both T 0 and R 1
Every Hausdorff space is also T 1
or Urysohn , if any two distinct points in X are separated by closed neighbourhoods
Every T 2½ space is also Hausdorff
is completely Hausdorff , or completely T 2 , if any two distinct points in X are separated by a continuous function
Every completely Hausdorff space is also T 2½
is regular if, given any point x and closed set F in X such that x does not belong to F , they are separated by neighbourhoods
Every regular space is also R 1
is regular Hausdorff , or T 3 , if it is both T 0 and regular
Every regular Hausdorff space is also T 2½
is completely regular if, given any point x and closed set F in X such that x does not belong to F , they are separated by a continuous function
Every completely regular space is also regular
is Tychonoff , or T 3½ , completely T 3 , or completely regular Hausdorff , if it is both T 0 and completely regular

annotations.label: Tychonoff implies regular Hausdorff and completely Hausdorff

anchor.snippet: Every Tychonoff space is both regular Hausdorff and completely Hausdorff
is normal if any two disjoint closed subsets of X are separated by neighbourhoods
a space is normal if and only if any two disjoint closed sets can be separated by a continuous function
is normal regular if it is both R 0 and normal
Every normal regular space is also completely regular
is normal Hausdorff , or T 4 , if it is both T 1 and normal

annotations.label: Normal Hausdorff implies Tychonoff and normal regular

anchor.snippet: Every normal Hausdorff space is also both Tychonoff and normal regular
is completely normal if any two separated sets are separated by neighbourhoods
Every completely normal space is also normal
is completely normal Hausdorff , or T 5 or completely T 4 , if it is both completely normal and T 1

annotations.label: Completely normal Hausdorff implies normal Hausdorff

anchor.snippet: Every completely normal Hausdorff space is also normal Hausdorff
is perfectly normal if any two disjoint closed sets are precisely separated by a continuous function

annotations.label: Perfectly normal implies completely normal and completely regular

anchor.snippet: Every perfectly normal space is also both completely normal and completely regular
is perfectly normal Hausdorff , or T 6 or perfectly T 4 , if it is both perfectly normal and T 0

annotations.label: Perfectly normal Hausdorff implies completely normal Hausdorff

anchor.snippet: Every perfectly normal Hausdorff space is also completely normal Hausdorff
is sober if, for every nonempty closed set C that is not the union of two of its closed proper subsets
Any Hausdorff space must be sober, and any sober space must be T 0
is weak Hausdorff if, for every continuous map f to X from a compact Hausdorff space, the image of f is closed in X

annotations.label: Hausdorff implies weak Hausdorff implies T1

anchor.snippet: Any Hausdorff space must be weak Hausdorff, and any weak Hausdorff space must be T 1
is a KC space if all compact subsets of X are closed

annotations.label: KC strictly between T1 and T2

anchor.snippet: The KC space property is strictly between T 1 and T 2 (Hausdorff) in strength
The KC space property is strictly
is semiregular if the regular open sets form a base for the open sets of X
Any regular space must also be semiregular
is quasi-regular if for any nonempty open set G , there is a nonempty open set H such that the closure of H is contained in G
is fully normal if every open cover has an open star refinement
is fully T 4 , or fully normal Hausdorff , if it is both T 1 and fully normal

annotations.label: Fully normal implies normal; fully T4 implies T4

anchor.snippet: Every fully normal space is normal and every fully T 4 space is T 4
one can show that every fully T 4 space is paracompact
for the cofinite topology on infinitely many points, which is T 1 , every subset is compact but not every subset is closed
for the cocountable topology on uncountably many points, the compact sets are all finite and hence all closed but the space is not T 2

annotations.label: Continuous image of compact Hausdorff in KC is closed

anchor.snippet: The image of a continuous map from a compact Hausdorff space is necessarily compact
